description: Realistic failure corpus: compact RTL designs with distinct failure mechanisms, each driven through the existing rtl-agent pipeline unchanged.

examples.name: fsm-sequencer
examples.description: An arm/drain sequencer where a premature second send while ARMED (a protocol violation) drives an illegal FSM transition that corrupts the held payload.

examples.name: fifo-underflow
examples.description: A small synchronous FIFO where popping (stall) while empty is an underflow that returns undefined read data instead of a safe zero.

examples.name: counter-overflow
examples.description: A saturating event counter where the boundary update corrupts the count instead of saturating, so exactly the four increments to the boundary are the irreducible failing core.
